title: Intesa Sanpaolo clôture son premier trimestre sur un bénéfice record, porté par sa branche Gestion de patrimoine
author: Na
description: Intesa Sanpaolo reported record Q1 2026 net income of €2.8 billion, up 6% year-over-year, with operating profit reaching €7.2 billion. The bank's Wealth Management division drove growth with €1.4 trillion in assets under management. The company maintained a strong cost-to-income ratio of 35.9%, achieved a 21% ROE and 25% ROTE, and confirmed its full-year 2026 net income guidance of €10 billion.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The bank delivered record quarterly results with strong year-over-year growth in net income, record operating profit, best-in-class cost-to-income ratio, robust capital position (CET1 >13%), and significant shareholder returns. Wealth Management growth and diversified revenue streams demonstrate resilience and strategic execution.

title: Intesa Sanpaolo meldet auf Vermögensverwaltung gestützten Rekordgewinn für das ersten Quartal
author: Na
description: Intesa Sanpaolo achieved record Q1 2026 net income of €2.8 billion (+6% YoY) with operating income of €7.2 billion. The bank demonstrated strong performance across wealth management (€1.4 trillion in client assets), fee-based revenues, and insurance operations, while maintaining industry-leading operational efficiency with a 35.9% cost-to-income ratio. The bank confirmed its €10 billion net income guidance for 2026 and plans to distribute €9.4 billion to shareholders.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Record quarterly net income of €2.8bn (+6% YoY), record operating income of €7.2bn, exceptional ROE of 21% and ROTE of 25%, industry-leading cost-to-income ratio of 35.9%, strong wealth management growth with €1.4 trillion in client assets, robust capital position (CET1 13%), low NPL ratio of 0.8%, and substantial shareholder returns of €9.4bn planned for 2026 all indicate strong financial health and operational excellence.

title: Intesa Sanpaolo posts record Q1 profit, driven by Wealth Management
author: Na
description: Intesa Sanpaolo delivered its best quarterly result in history with €2.8 billion net income in Q1 2026, up 6% year-on-year. Operating income reached a record €7.2 billion driven by balanced growth across net interest income, commissions, and insurance activities. The bank achieved its lowest-ever cost/income ratio of 35.9%, maintained a strong CET1 ratio above 13%, and plans to return €9.4 billion to shareholders in 2026.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The bank reported record quarterly net income of €2.8 billion (+6% YoY), record operating income of €7.2 billion, lowest-ever cost/income ratio of 35.9%, strong capital position (CET1 >13%), best-in-class profitability metrics (ROE 21%, ROTE 25%), and substantial shareholder returns of €9.4 billion planned for 2026. All key performance indicators show significant improvement and strength.

title: Infrastructures : IMI CIB prône le dialogue à Londres autour du plan britannique de 846 milliards d’euros
author: Mauro Micillo
description: Intesa Sanpaolo's IMI CIB division hosted a conference in London discussing the UK's €846 billion infrastructure investment plan, focusing on sustainable infrastructure, energy transition, and social/environmental improvements.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Mentioned in context of acquisition by Macquarie Asset Management, no explicit positive or negative implications
Actively supporting infrastructure investments, participating in international transactions, and demonstrating commitment to sustainable transformation

title: Infrastruktur: IMI CIB fördert in London den Dialog über den Plan des Vereinigten Königreichs in Höhe von 846 Mrd. EUR
author: Mauro Micillo
description: Intesa Sanpaolo's IMI Corporate & Investment Banking division hosted a conference in London discussing the UK's 10-year infrastructure plan, which involves €846 billion in investments focused on infrastructure, energy transition, and social/ecological system improvements.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Mentioned in context of transmission acquisition by Macquarie Asset Management
Actively supporting sustainable infrastructure investments and playing a strategic role in facilitating dialogue between public and private sectors
